channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: PTI Questions Centre on Handling of Terrorism in Balochistan and KP
item.description: On Friday, PTI lawmakers in the National Assembly raised serious questions about the rising terrorism and law and order issues in Balochistan, pointing out what they described as a double standard by ...

item.title: Canadian VPN Company Considers Relocation Over Proposed Data Retention Law
item.description: Toronto-based Windscribe, a Canadian company that provides online privacy and security tools including a VPN service, has indicated it may leave Canada due to Bill C-22. The proposed legislation could...

item.title: Dama Wa Spares Shares Tips on Building a Successful Business from Scratch
item.description: Kenyan social media influencer and entrepreneur Dama Wa Spares has opened up about her journey to building a successful business despite limited financial resources. In a recent video from China, wher...

item.title: Suspect in Senate shooting incident charged with firearms violations and direct assault
item.description: Inaresto ng mga awtoridad ang isang volunteer ng National Bureau of Investigation (NBI), na kilala sa alyas na “Mel Oragon,” kasunod ng insidente ng pamamaril sa loob ng Senate premises noong May 13. ...

item.title: U.S. Passengers Quarantined After Hantavirus Outbreak on Cruise Ship
item.description: Following a hantavirus outbreak on the M/V Hondius cruise ship, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) are monitoring 41 people in the U.S. due to potential exposure. Eighteen repatriate...

item.title: Carolyn Wood Sherif: Pioneer of Feminist Psychology and Advocate Against Scientific Bias
item.description: Carolyn Wood Sherif was a pioneering psychologist whose work significantly influenced both social and feminist psychology, yet her contributions were historically overlooked. In 1954, she co-led the f...

item.title: China expresses willingness to assist in easing Strait of Hormuz tensions
item.description: The article reports on Chinese President Xi Jinping offering assistance to facilitate the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, a crucial shipping route for global oil transport. The announcement comes a...

item.title: Ruto and Macron Co-Host France-Africa Summit in Nairobi, Boosting Economic Ties
item.description: Kenya recently hosted a significant France-Africa summit in Nairobi, co-chaired by President William Ruto and French President Emmanuel Macron. About 30 African heads of state, over 1,500 business lea...

item.title: Ottawa Provides $20M to Support Southwestern Ontario Businesses Affected by Tariffs
item.description: The federal government has announced an investment of more than $20 million to support businesses in southwestern Ontario that have been affected by recent tariff measures. This funding is part of a b...

item.title: Look Mum No Computer: UK musician aiming for Eurovision success
item.description: The article intended to introduce the UK musician known as 'Look Mum No Computer', highlighting his unique electronic music style and his aspirations to represent the United Kingdom at the Eurovision ...

item.title: Ghana Approves Evacuation of 300 Citizens Amid Xenophobic Violence in South Africa
item.description: The Government of Ghana has approved the immediate evacuation of 300 Ghanaians from South Africa following a fresh wave of xenophobic attacks targeting black immigrants. The announcement was made by F...

item.title: Growing Interest in Carbon Trading as 90 Tanzanian Firms Join Emerging Green Economy
item.description: In Tanzania, the green economy sector is continuing to expand, with at least 90 companies now registered to participate in carbon trading initiatives. This development reflects a growing awareness amo...

item.title: Managing Hantavirus Outbreak: Lessons Learned from Covid Communications
item.description: A recent outbreak of the Andes strain of hantavirus on the MV Hondius cruise ship in the Atlantic has revived public anxiety reminiscent of the Covid-19 pandemic. Though hantavirus is not new and unli...

item.title: Israel and Lebanon Agree to Extend Ceasefire Amid Ongoing Tensions
item.description: Israel and Lebanon don extend their ceasefire by 45 days even as fresh strikes dey continue, especially for southern Lebanon where Israeli forces dey target Hezbollah. The talks wey happen for Washing...

item.title: IMF projects Pakistan’s federal revenue at Rs17.1 trillion with new budgetary measures for FY27
item.description: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has set Pakistan’s federal revenue target at Rs17.145 trillion for the fiscal year 2026-27. This comes with proposed new budgetary measures worth Rs430 billion an...

item.title: Unable to access article: News Corp Australia blocked the page
item.description: The article could not be accessed because News Corp Australia detected what it believes to be crawler bot traffic. The message advises that users who are not bots should try disabling AdBlockers, pop-...

item.title: Forest performance shows the importance of theatre in nurturing children’s creativity and cultural participation
item.description: Young children in Australia are spending less time outdoors, despite research showing that time in natural settings supports physical and mental health, social skills, resilience, and learning. Polygl...

item.title: Five suspects appear in Pretoria court for Senzo Meyiwa murder trial
item.description: The trial for the murder of South African soccer star Senzo Meyiwa has resumed at the Pretoria High Court. Meyiwa, who was the celebrated captain of Bafana Bafana, was tragically shot and killed in 20...

item.title: Trump Reaffirms He Does Not Consider Americans’ Financial Situations When Discussing Iran
item.description: President Donald Trump recently doubled down on remarks he made regarding Americans’ financial situations in the context of international diplomacy. Earlier this week, during his departure for a state...

item.title: BJP protests at CM Omar Abdullah’s residence demanding liquor prohibition in Kashmir Valley
item.description: On Friday, BJP workers staged a protest outside the residence of Jammu & Kashmir Chief Minister Omar Abdullah, urging the government to impose a prohibition on liquor in the Kashmir Valley. Altaf Thak...
